name: IPEC Guidelines

questions.text: Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?

guidance.title: Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?
guidance.risk: For governance-and-compliance-q1, this control improves auditability for insurance compliance and board-level financial governance accountability by proving that 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' is governed, executed, and reviewable within Governance & Compliance.
actions: In Governance & Compliance, close gap governance-and-compliance-q1 by formalizing control execution for 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?'. Document accountable ownership, implementation milestones, and operating cadence aligned to insurance-sector governance, policyholder protection, and prudential reporting expectations from IPEC.
evidence: Approved procedure or standard mapped to governance-and-compliance-q1 and section 'Governance & Compliance'.
Dated operating records showing execution of 'Is there a documented policy for governance & compliance?' over the latest review cycle.
Management or committee evidence confirming oversight decisions and remediation tracking for governance-and-compliance-q1.
